focus: Ghana tax-benefit surface: national income tax (PAYE and self-assessment), personal reliefs, social contributions, and cash-transfer / social-protection programmes.
purpose: Household-level comparison-oracle and supporting-source references for Ghana Axiom coverage. None of these are legal authority.
oracle_scope: A RuleSpec oracle must be an executable, pinned external calculator that accepts household-level inputs and returns household-level tax-benefit outputs comparable to Axiom outputs. Aggregate simulators, distributional reports, parameter documentation, and public model summaries are supporting references only, not oracles.

oracles.name: GHAMOD
oracles.dataset: gh_2013_a8.txt, gh_2017_a8.txt (GLSS-based input microdata shipped in the bundle)
oracles.role: Intended household-level tax-benefit comparison oracle for Ghana. GHAMOD is the SOUTHMOD tax-benefit microsimulation model for Ghana (UNU-WIDER, in collaboration with the University of Ghana, the Ghana Statistical Service, and the Ministry of Finance / GRA). Built on the EUROMOD platform.

availability_check.detail: UNU-WIDER approved the SOUTHMOD access request on 2026-07-07 and the model bundle (SOUTHMOD A4.0, sha256 above) with Ghana input datasets and DRDs is held locally under the Adhesion Agreement (personal links; non-redistributable; the bundle and datasets must never be committed or shared). GHAMOD runs on the EUROMOD engine and is now wired as the Ghana household oracle by per-case parity suites in TheAxiomFoundation/axiom-oracles, run live on system GH_2025: gh-income-tax-rate-schedule compares the encoded Act 1111 First Schedule rate schedule (resident_individual_income_tax) against GHAMOD tin_s, and gh-personal-reliefs compares the Act 896 Fifth Schedule reliefs against GHAMOD's per-relief tinta0X_s outputs. The rate schedule matches to the cedi for chargeable income at or below 605,000; at 700,000 the encoded statutory 35% top band diverges by 4,750 because GHAMOD omits it (dispositioned oracle gap, Axiom correct). All eight relief cases match to the cedi (old age 1,500, disability 25% of the base, child education 600 per child capped at 3, aged dependant 1,000 per relative capped at 2, dependent spouse 1,200, and a combined household); the training relief and the para 1(a) lone-parent limb are documented oracle gaps with no live case (GHAMOD does not model them). Pending Nikhil's review of the oracle wiring.

oracles.systems: GH_2013 through GH_2025 (13 policy years; Act 1111 rate schedule in GH_2024/GH_2025, Act 1007 reliefs from GH_2020)
